text: Artificial intelligence is changing how we think about creativity. Today, AI can generate paintings, compose music, and even write stories or poems. Some of this work is impressive and can look or sound as if it were made by a person. However, there is an important difference: human creativity often comes from personal experience, emotion, or intention. AI does not have feelings or a life story—it simply learns from patterns in existing data. While AI can copy styles or combine ideas in new ways, many people argue that true creativity requires human consciousness. Still, AI can be a useful tool for artists and writers, helping them explore new possibilities or overcome creative blocks.
